response.justification: The candidate meets the minimum bar — a technical bachelor's degree and roughly 19 years of hands-on software engineering across web, embedded, low-latency, and infrastructure work — and shows entrepreneurial drive through founding GISCollective. However, the role is a director-level AI strategy position, and the resume shows a senior/staff IC trajectory without evidence of organization-level technical leadership, strategic customer advisory work, or recognized AI expertise. AI exposure is recent and tooling-oriented (MCP code generators, agent infrastructure) rather than demonstrated AI products or platforms, making the fit partial.
strengths: 19+ years of broad, hands-on engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput systems
Founder experience with a long-running open-source geo-data platform used in production
Recent practical work on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP developer tooling
concerns: No demonstrated director/principal-level scope or engineering leadership of large teams or strategy
AI experience is recent and shallow relative to the 'recognized AI expertise' preference
No advanced degree in an AI-related field and no visible cloud-platform strategy background

key_factors.factor: Long, diverse engineering track record with founder experience
